markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define safe rollback/repair rules and migration tests for every table family added by WP-009. This MT belongs to PostgresEventLedgerCore. Build the durable canonical storage and event foundation for project knowledge, editor state, and memory.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
All storage MTs must fail closed if PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority is unavailable.
No in-memory, SQLite, markdown, fixture-only, or UI-state authority may satisfy closeout.
scope.expected_behavior: RollbackAndRepairMigrations leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: RollbackAndRepairMigrations is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 5fe02b12; PostgresEventLedgerCore group: 47 tests across 8 binaries green on Handshake-managed PostgreSQL (port 5544, no Docker/SQLite). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: The scratch-schema rollback proof asserts ZERO leftover only for information_schema BASE TABLE (test:217-221); it does NOT assert zero leftover FUNCTIONS (information_schema.routines). The down files DO drop all 4 trigger functions (knowledge_edge_requires_span, knowledge_edge_span_delete_guard, knowledge_claim_requires_span, knowledge_passage_requires_evidence - verified 1:1 against the up files), so the actual rollback IS complete; but a FUTURE down file that forgot a DROP FUNCTION would still pass this test green. Add a routines-empty assertion to make the 'zero knowledge objects incl functions' claim test-enforced, not just construction-true.

The scratch chain stops at 0142 by design (CRDT 0150+/ingestion 0160+ FK into knowledge_sources and must roll back first in a full DB); this is documented and acceptable for the group's deterministic proof, but means the full-chain rollback ordering is not exercised here.
evasion_probes: Verified rollback completeness by inventory: NO CREATE TYPE (all enums are CHECK-constrained TEXT) and NO sequences (all ids TEXT) exist in 0130-0142, so functions are the only non-table objects; triggers are auto-dropped with their tables (CASCADE). Down files drop child->parent in FK-safe order and clean knowledge_schema_registry rows.
every_knowledge_migration_ships_a_down_file asserts each NNNN_knowledge*.sql has a .down.sql twin containing DROP, >=13 checked (test:59-99) - filesystem audit, always runs.

scratch_schema_apply_rollback_reapply (real PG): apply full chain ->=14 tables, rollback 0142->0130 leaves zero knowledge_ tables, re-apply restores EXACT table set AND exact registry rows (test:119-251) - repair path proven, scratch schema disposed.
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING registry inserts make re-apply idempotent.
evidence_checked: knowledge_migration_tests.rs:59-251 (down-file audit, scratch apply/rollback/reapply, registry equality)
migrations 0130-0142 .down.sql files (verified DROP coverage: 4/4 functions, all tables, registry cleanup, FK-safe order)

hardening_pending.reason: rollback completeness: add information_schema.routines zero-leftover assertion after rollback.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
